title: Accounts Associate
description:
Role Description:

Reporting into the Accounting Manager, and supported by the Accounting Senior, the Accounts Associate provides assistance and gives administrative support in maintaining the accuracy of the accounting records of the client. The Accounts Associate provides support by performing financial accounting and corporate reporting activities; and processing business transactions and reports using information technology tools.


Responsibilities:

  • Perform the accounting function in preparing full sets of accounts which includes maintaining/keying in data into accounting systems/software.
  • Record clients transactions and events relating to sales, purchases, receivables, payable and cash; as well as inventory accruals, prepayments, capital structure and finance costs; ensuring the accuracy of these financial records and statements.
  • Assist with the preparation of month-end financial statement, cash flow statements, bank reconciliation statements and schedules to the accounts.
  • Prepare payments to vendors; this includes reviewing invoices for appropriate documentation and approval prior to payment, matching the invoices to cheques, obtaining all signatures for cheques, preparing the payment vouchers, updating the Cash Flow Listing through cheques/telegraphic transfers/bank drafts, distributing the cheques accordingly.
  • Process payroll transactions into the accounting systems/software.
  • Prepare tax returns for submissions and classifying allowable expenses accurately.
  • Prepare the Annual Survey for Clients.
  • Prepare statutory contribution (e.g. Central Provident Fund) calculations.
  • Act in accordance with the Code of Professional Conduct and Ethics for Accountants and Accounting Entities, and International Financial Reporting Standards when providing accounting services.
  • Identify ethical conflicts in the work environment.
  • Ensure compliance with the firm's quality control procedures.
  • Be a team player, working cooperatively and communicating effectively in the work environment.
  • Identify areas for personal development to improve work performance.

Desired Skills and Qualifications:

  • Diploma, Degree in Accountancy and or Pursuing Professional Accounting Qualification
  • Fluent in written and spoken English
  • MS Office Suite and accounting software (e.g. Xero, SAGE)

Thank you for expressing your interest to join us. We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be shortlisted for an interview.

postedCompany.description:
Who we are today is the collective effort of challengers, changemakers and goal-driven leaders. Our roots extend deep into our vision of providing client-centric services and our legacy is etched in the very essence of the evolving landscape of businesses in Singapore and beyond.


Kreston Helmi Talib is an independent member of the Kreston Global Network. A collaborative community of 27,000+ professionals across 160 independent accounting firms in over 114 countries, the network's legacy, span for over 50 years, rooted in the unwavering commitment of each of its members to helping businesses and individuals thrive on an international stage


The team at Kreston Helmi Talib Accounting & Advisory Pte Ltd hold expertise in a full suite of Accounting and Business Process Services, including Accounting and Bookkeeping, Drafting Financial Statements and Payment Processing.

Long-Standing Local Firm

In three decades, the Helmi Talib Group has grown to become one of the top 30 accounting firms based in Singapore.


We offer a wide range of services to an expanding pool of both local and international clientele. Our services include:

  • Preparation of Accounts and Bookkeeping (Xero, Sage, and other Accounting Softwares)
  • Assistance with Internal Reporting and Management Reports Preparation
  • Disbursement Processing
  • Drafting Statutory Financial Statements
  • Conversion of Statutory Financial Statements to XBRL
  • Cash Flow Projection
